title: M.Sc (Hort.) Fruit Science
content: M.Sc. (Hort.) Fruit Science is a two year post graduate programme which involves the study of plant harvesting, growing trees and landscape architecture of fruit trees. It also includes breeding, storage, packaging and shipment of fruit crops. B.Sc. (Hons) Agriculture/ related degrees with minimum 60% marks for general and 55% for SC/ ST/ PH/ other reserved category candidates or equivalent CGPA Students having CUET Score will be given preference in merit. 01 Equip graduates with the knowledge and skills to identify and solve complex problems in horticultural production using scientific, technological, and data-driven approaches. 02 Foster awareness of societal, environmental, health, legal, and cultural considerations to ensure sustainable and ethically sound horticultural solutions. 03 Encourage critical thinking, creativity, and perseverance in developing and implementing effective solutions to challenges in horticulture. 04 Prepare graduates to work effectively as individuals, team members, or leaders within diverse and multidisciplinary agribusiness and research environments. 05 Uphold integrity and moral responsibility in the practice, teaching, and research of horticultural and agricultural sciences. 01 Employ horticultural science expertise to the resolution of farming-related problems. 02 Have knowledge and aptitude for problem-solving and critical thinking towards horticulture production 03 Develop and use the most appropriate fruit production methods, resources, and IT tools while being aware of their constraints. 04 Assess societal, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues, as well as the resulting duties, in relation to the professional practice of horticulture. 05 Recognize the effects of expert horticultural solutions in the context of society and the environment for sustainable development. 06 Develop into tenacious individuals who challenge, practice, and use the knowledge gained to develop solutions to problems in horticultural production. 07 Effectively perform on a personal level, as a team player or leader in a varied agribusiness organization, and in multidisciplinary situations. 08 Incorporate moral principles to teaching and research in the various agricultural science fields. 01 Get the in-depth knowledge of plants with good production potential and rootstock tolerance. 02 Demonstrate scientific skills for maintaining high yielding varieties and nutrient management in horticultural crops. 03 01 To demonstrate written and oral communication (PSO1). 02 To understand the basic concept of crop production technologies in fruit crops (PSO2). 03 To collect data, analyze data in solving problems of farmers by conducting applied research in horticulture (PSO3). 04 Apply their research expertise in recommending good agriculture practices in fruits for enhancing production and income of the farming communities. B.Sc. (Hons) Agriculture/ related degrees with minimum 60% marks for general and 55% for SC/ ST/ PH/ other reserved category candidates or equivalent CGPA Students having CUET Score will be given preference in merit.
